message:
perf(indexer): skip redundant final checkpoint refresh when rebuild already persisted exact counts; close readonly rebuilds without checkpoint

Two related shutdown-path optimizations on top of the prior
single-writer collapse. The new logic also re-splits the
last_scan_ts / last_indexed_at writes back into two ephemeral
writers — that's incidental to wedging the new "did the rebuild
already persist exact state?" check between them, not a revert
of the collapse.

1. Authoritative checkpoint is persisted by `rebuild_tantivy_from_db`,
   not re-written at shutdown
   `LexicalRebuildOutcome` gains a new field:
       pub exact_checkpoint_persisted: bool
   `rebuild_tantivy_from_db` now, on the happy path immediately
   before returning, hydrates `rebuild_state` with the exact post-
   rebuild counts (`total_messages = observed_messages`,
   `committed_offset = total_conversations`,
   `processed_conversations`, `indexed_docs`),
   `mark_completed(index_meta_fingerprint(&index_path)?)`s it,
   `persist_lexical_rebuild_state(&index_path, &rebuild_state)`s it,
   and returns `exact_checkpoint_persisted: true`. The early-return
   "already-completed" arm returns `false`. Every callsite that
   constructs the outcome (the four rebuild paths in `run_index`)
   captures the bool into a function-local
   `exact_completed_lexical_checkpoint`.
   In `run_index`'s shutdown tail, after the
   `set_last_indexed_at` write, the code now branches:
       if exact_completed_lexical_checkpoint && exact_total_counts.is_some() {
           tracing::info!(... "skipping final lexical checkpoint refresh
                              because the authoritative rebuild already
                              persisted exact completed state");
       } else {
           refresh_completed_lexical_rebuild_checkpoint_for_final_state(...)?;
       }
   Result: on the common case (a rebuild we just finished), we
   don't pay another checkpoint-refresh that would only rewrite
   the same data with potentially less-precise progress-derived
   counts. The fallback branch keeps the old behavior for paths
   that didn't go through a real rebuild (resume-only, no-op
   incremental, etc.).

2. `storage.close()` → `storage.close_without_checkpoint()` on the
   two readonly Tantivy-rebuild close sites
   The two `storage.close().with_context(...)` calls in
   `rebuild_tantivy_from_db` (the early-return "already complete"
   path and the post-rebuild close) become
   `storage.close_without_checkpoint().with_context(...)`. The
   storage was opened readonly for the rebuild, so there's nothing
   in the WAL to drain — paying for the checkpoint just slows
   down shutdown. Context strings are updated to mention "without
   checkpoint" so the failure-path tracing is honest about what
   it skipped.

3. About the writer split
   The previous commit (4fa8f884) collapsed the final
   `set_last_scan_ts` + `set_last_indexed_at` writes into one
   ephemeral writer to amortize open/preflight/close cost. This
   commit splits them again — *not* to undo the perf win, but
   because the new shutdown logic wants to:
     - persist `last_scan_ts` (only when `performed_scan`)
     - then derive `exact_total_counts =
       exact_total_counts_from_progress(opts.progress.as_ref())`
     - then persist `last_indexed_at` and *conditionally* call
       `refresh_completed_lexical_rebuild_checkpoint_for_final_state`
       based on `exact_completed_lexical_checkpoint &&
       exact_total_counts.is_some()`.
   Carrying the `exact_total_counts` derivation and the new
   conditional inside one writer closure was awkward (the closure
   would have to take the progress option, do the count
   derivation, and decide whether to call the next external
   function — all under a write lock that doesn't need to span
   any of those decisions). Splitting back into two short writers
   keeps the new branch readable. Net cost vs. the collapse: one
   extra open/preflight/close on shutdown — strictly less than
   the redundant `refresh_completed_lexical_rebuild_checkpoint`
   call this commit avoids on the common path.

Tests
  Two test sites in `mod tests` now assert
  `rebuild.exact_checkpoint_persisted` is true after a real
  rebuild, plus the second test is extended with full
  load-and-verify of the post-rebuild `load_lexical_rebuild_checkpoint`
  shape: `completed`, `total_messages: 4`,
  `processed_conversations: 2`, `committed_offset: 2`,
  `indexed_docs: 4`. Pins the new authoritative-checkpoint
  contract.
